name: Niagara Rim Trail
description: A scenic high-desert rim trail with panoramic views of the Eastern Sierra. Best run in late spring through fall. Some rocky sections near the north end.

name: Deadman Summit Loop
description: A challenging loop through volcanic terrain near Mammoth. High clearance required. Stunning views of the Mono Basin from the summit.

name: Slick Rock Jeep Trail
location: Lake Alpine to Utica Reservoir, CA
description: Slick Rock Road (F.R. 7N17) is a moderate to difficult 4x4 trail that begins at Lake Alpine, skirts the Carson Iceberg Wilderness and ends at Utica Reservoir after crossing multiple granite slabs, rock piles, boulders, and alpine creeks. In 2018, a reroute project added 0.75 miles and two new rock crawling obstacles. Street-legal 4x4 vehicles only — no bypasses on difficult sections. High clearance, 4WD, oversized tires, low range, lockers, and rock sliders recommended. No fee. No water on trail.
notes: During winter, Highway 4 or Spicer Road may be closed making this an out-and-back. Trail is only open to wheeled motorized use when at least one access road is open. If both are closed, only tracked over-snow vehicles permitted. Verify conditions before running — trail may require post-winter cleanup and USFS approval. Call Calaveras Ranger Station: (209) 795-1381.
adoptedBy: Joaquin Jeepers — Stockton, CA. Adopted trail maintainers since the early 1960s.
parking: Ample parking at both ends. Information kiosk with map at Lake Alpine end.
restrooms: Marmot Day Use Area (Lake Alpine side), Rocky Point Campground / Utica Reservoir end.
statusNote: Currently closed. Verify current conditions with Calaveras Ranger Station (209) 795-1381 before running. Early season may require post-winter cleanup and USFS approval.
